title: The Snyder Cut Will Be Released! But Why? Plus: How to Watch 100 Classic Movies.
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins react to the confirmation of the Snyder Cut, then work through a mailbag on Peacock, HBO Max, and how to start watching classic movies.

segments.heading: The Snyder Cut Will Be Released

films.title: Justice League
films.note: The unfinished Joss Whedon version everyone's arguing about.

films.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice
films.note: Sean: has "a couple of things to recommend," mainly Affleck.

films.title: Man of Steel
films.note: Sean says he never got it and calls it one of the worst theater experiences he's had.
Cited as Snyder doing effective, visceral action filmmaking.

films.title: Dawn of the Dead
films.note: Snyder's remake, cited alongside 300 as his strongest work.

films.title: The Magnificent Ambersons
films.note: Orson Welles's lost longer cut is Sean's model for a version worth chasing.

films.title: Citizen Kane
films.note: Referenced for Welles's post-Kane battles with the studio.

films.title: Hard Eight
films.note: Paul Thomas Anderson's debut, recut and retitled by the studio from his original "Sydney."

films.title: All the Pretty Horses
films.note: Amanda's pick: Billy Bob Thornton's cut was taken away and "really butchered."

films.title: Apocalypse Now
films.note: The "Redux" footage-restored recut is discussed against Coppola's later "Final Cut."

films.title: Blade Runner
films.note: Cited for its many competing cuts.

films.title: Kingdom of Heaven
films.note: Ridley Scott's extended cut, a favorite of Chris Ryan and Jason Concepcion.

films.title: The Goonies
films.note: Amanda's bar for what would make the Snyder Cut worth it.

films.title: Cats
films.note: Bobby's joke reference to the infamous unfinished-CGI "butthole cut" briefly sent to some theaters.

segments.heading: The Mailbag: Peacock, HBO Max, and What's Next
segments.note: Oscars, AMC/Amazon, streaming premieres, and TV shows that could've been movies

films.title: Wonder Woman 1984
films.note: Debated as a candidate to skip theaters for a VOD release.

films.title: Sex, Lies, and Videotape
films.note: News that Soderbergh wrote a quarantine sequel; Amanda wants the original cast back.

films.title: The Maltese Falcon
films.note: Sean's pick for the first thing he'll watch on HBO Max.

films.title: El Camino: A Breaking Bad Movie
films.note: The one Breaking Bad entry that actually played in theaters.

films.title: Da 5 Bloods
films.note: Reacting to the trailer; Sean is in for Delroy Lindo and the Chambers Brothers needle drop.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: Amanda's example of a movie that deepens on rewatch, via Greta Gerwig's adaptation.

films.title: Inception
films.note: Amanda's example of the opposite: great on first watch, less rewarding after.

films.title: Dunkirk
films.note: Sean's Dunkirk rewatchables appearance changed his read on Nolan's strengths and weaknesses.

films.title: The Ballad of Buster Scruggs
films.note: Cited by a listener as a favorite anthology film.

films.title: Wild Tales
films.note: Cited alongside Buster Scruggs as a favorite anthology.

films.title: Trick 'r Treat
films.note: Sean's anthology-horror recommendation.

films.title: Creepshow
films.note: George Romero and Stephen King's anthology, called a classic.

films.title: V/H/S
films.note: Named as the more mumblecore, low-budget end of horror anthologies.

films.title: New York Stories
films.note: Sean singles out Scorsese's segment, "Life Lessons," as a banger.

films.title: Coffee and Cigarettes
films.note: Jim Jarmusch's anthology, cited for the RZA/Bill Murray segment.

films.title: Fantasia
films.note: Sean's pick for a wonderful animated anthology.

films.title: The Lovebirds
films.note: Sold by Paramount to Netflix; teased as next week's review.

films.title: Greyhound
films.note: Sony's Tom Hanks naval film, sold to Apple TV+.

films.title: West Side Story
films.note: Referenced as the higher-profile Spielberg film moved to a streamer, unlike Greyhound.

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: Sean wonders aloud whether it marked the end of the mega-event-movie era.

segments.heading: Plus: How to Watch 100 Classic Movies
segments.note: a listener asks how to start with classic cinema

films.title: The Lion in Winter
films.note: Sean's pick for an underrated pre-1970 movie, with Peter O'Toole and Katharine Hepburn.

films.title: The Big Heat
films.note: Amanda just rewatched this Fritz Lang noir on the Criterion Channel.

films.title: The 39 Steps
films.note: A listener's prompt: the Criterion commentary added a lot to the experience.

films.title: Boogie Nights
films.note: Sean's favorite commentary track, PT Anderson narrating his Scorsese influences.

films.title: Gone Girl
films.note: Amanda's favorite commentary, David Fincher on the film and on Affleck.

films.title: The Wizard of Oz
films.note: Sean's answer for the movie that made him love movies.

films.title: Mary Poppins
films.note: Amanda's childhood equivalent of The Wizard of Oz.

films.title: When Harry Met Sally...
films.note: Amanda cites it as showing her how smart a movie's thinking could be.

films.title: The Social Network
films.note: Amanda's other formative pick, a movie that's "really pop and also excellent."

films.title: Reservoir Dogs
films.note: Sean on the instant academia that sprang up around Tarantino after this.

films.title: In the Soup
films.note: Sean's example of a mid-'90s indie he had to hunt down after reading about it.

films.title: Mutiny on the Bounty
films.note: Sean discovered Charles Laughton via the AFI 100 list as a teenager.

TV: What We Do in the Shadows

Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice.title: Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice

El Camino: A Breaking Bad Movie.title: El Camino: A Breaking Bad Movie
